'''Palo Torshan''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Major/Legends|major]] and one of the top security personnel at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] headquarters on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. He developed counterintelligence tactics at Echo Base, including setting loose the flamboyant [[Color/Legends|red]] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[R-3PO/Legends|R-3PO]] in search of [[Imperial espionage droid|Imperial spy droids]]. Torshan formerly served under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] and sat in on the conferences planning the Rebellion's assault on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] for the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

==Biography==
[[Major/Legends|Major]] Palo Torshan was an [[Alliance Military/Legends|officer]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> He<ref name="Blog" /> served under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] at<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> the Rebel Alliance [[Great Temple/Legends|headquarters]]<ref name="Who's Who">{{InsiderCite|74|Who's Who in Echo Base: The Unsung Rebels of the Battle of Hoth}}</ref> on [[Yavin 4]] and attended the strategy conferences planning the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> in which the Rebels assaulted and destroyed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Torshan later continued to serve with [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

[[File:PaloTorshanTauntaun.png|thumb|left|200px|Palo Torshan (left) stands beside a tauntaun in Echo Base.]]
Torshan coordinated Rebel security for Echo Base and developed counterintelligence techniques.<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> He suggested that the Rebels [[Programmer/Legends|reprogram]] [[R-3PO/Legends|R-3PO]], one of Echo Base's more temperamental and troublesome [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]], and set it loose to search for [[Imperial espionage droid]]s. Torshan also suggested not giving R-3PO a [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory wipe]] or repainting its [[Color/Legends|red]] body, since no one would suspect such a flamboyant and moody [[Droid/Legends|droid]] of being a [[Spy/Legends|spy]]. R-3PO proceeded to foil several Imperial infiltration attempts at Echo Base.<ref name="Who's Who" />

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|V}}, which includes Palo Torshan's service at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> just before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Torshan was present in Echo Base's [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]] stable on the [[Standard day/Legends|night]] Rebel [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] went missing during a routine [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] mission of the base perimeter.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> As Torshan and another [[Rebel trooper/Legends|soldier]]<ref name="Action Figure Archive">''[[Star Wars: The Action Figure Archive]]''</ref> were struggling with all their physical strength to calm one of the agitated tauntauns that was trying to break free,<ref name="ESB novel">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]]</ref> [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] passed their position and departed the base in search of Skywalker.<ref name="ESB" />

==Personality and traits==
Palo Torshan's instincts concerning R-3PO's value as a [[Espionage droid|spy droid]] proved correct after R-3PO snuffed out several Imperial infiltration attempts at Echo Base and, later, with Alliance High Command.<ref name="Who's Who" /> Torshan also possessed a certain level of [[Force-attunement]]. A [[Human/Legends|Human]], he had light skin.<ref name="Special Edition Limited" />

==Equipment==
During his service at Echo Base, Torshan donned the [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|cold-weather armor]] standard of the Rebel personnel on Hoth. His [[Armor/Legends|outfit]] included the set's [[Alliance Cold Weather Headgear|headgear]], [[Anti-glare goggles/Legends|anti-glare goggles]], [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|padded vest]], [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|lightweight tunic]],<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|gloves]],<ref name="ESB" /> [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|leggings]], and [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|boots]].<ref name="Action Figure Archive" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:RichardBonehill.png|thumb|right|217px|Actor Richard Bonehill outside ''The Empire Strikes Back'' set]]
Palo Torshan appeared in the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. He struggles with a wild tauntaun during the scene in which Han Solo departs Echo Base to search for the missing Luke Skywalker.<ref name="ESB" /> Actor [[Richard Bonehill]] portrayed the character in the film,<ref name="Richard Bonehill">{{WebCite|url=http://www.convention-guests.com/html/richard_bonehill.html|text=Richard Bonehill|work=Convention-Guests|archivedate=20100411072040}} See also [https://web.archive.org/web/20131219015534/http://convention-guests.com/assets/images/Richard-Bonehill-ESB.jpg image].</ref> uncredited.<ref name="ESB" /> Bonehill, who also played several other masked characters in both ''The Empire Strikes Back'' and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], originally filled in as Han Solo's tauntaun handler in place of actor [[Kit Hillier]],<ref name="Richard Bonehill" /> whom actor [[Harrison Ford]]—who played Solo—personally replaced during film rehearsal because of Hillier's inability to hold onto the mechanical tauntaun's reins.<ref name="Kit Hillier YouTube">{{YouTube|video=79dz9WqH9Ys|text=Kit Hillier TaunTaun Hoth Rebel ESB|channel=@ConGuests|name=ConGuests}}</ref>

The Torshan character was first named and given a brief backstory on the [http://www.starwarsccg.org/cardlists/images/starwars/SpecialEdition-Light/large/majorpalotorshan.gif "Major Palo Torshan" card] as part of the [[November]] [[1998]] ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]. Torshan's card identifies the character as "[[The Force/Legends|Force]]-attuned." This level of Force ability is a step down from a "[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]" and significantly less than a full [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]/[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] character for gameplay.<ref name="Special Edition Limited" />
